description: Admin Monitoring Dashboard (Fintech) â€” Live + 24h + 7d ops dashboard at admin.instnews.net/monitoring covering per-source ingestion latency, AI pipeline health, upstream API headroom (X rate-limit, Polygon, EDGAR, Stripe), and authoritative AWS + X API cost tracking. EMF-only metric emission (no new infra), uPlot charts, admin-only behind existing VPN + internal ALB. 13 stories: 1 metrics helper, 1 IAM/CDK update, 3 producer instrumentations, 2 admin APIs, 1 dashboard scaffold, 4 panels, 1 anomaly-badge polish. Source PRD: tasks/prd-admin-monitoring-dashboard.md

userStories.title: EMF metrics helper (app/services/metrics.py)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single Python helper that emits a CloudWatch EMF log line so producers across the app can publish metrics without duplicating the EMF JSON schema.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/metrics.py defines emit_metric(namespace, metric_name, value, unit='Count', dimensions=None, **extra_fields) that prints one valid EMF JSON line to stdout
EMF payload conforms to spec: top-level _aws.Timestamp (ms), _aws.CloudWatchMetrics with Namespace/Dimensions/Metrics[{Name,Unit}], dimension values at top level, extra_fields merged
emit_metrics(namespace, metrics=[{name,value,unit}], dimensions, **extra) variant for multi-metric single-line emission
Context manager timed(namespace, metric_name, dimensions=...) measures wall time and emits on exit
tests/services/test_metrics.py validates JSON shape, dimension handling, and the context manager
mypy app/services/metrics.py exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for all later producer stories. Keep log volume low via multi-metric variant.
test_assertions: python3 -c "from app.services.metrics import emit_metric; emit_metric('SIG', 'FeedLatency', 1.5, 'Seconds', {'Source':'SeekingAlpha'})" exits 0 and prints valid JSON

python3 -c "import json, contextlib; from io import StringIO; from app.services.metrics import emit_metric; buf=StringIO()\nwith contextlib.redirect_stdout(buf): emit_metric('X','Y',1.0,'Count',{'A':'b'})\nd=json.loads(buf.getvalue().strip()); assert '_aws' in d and d['A']=='b' and d['Y']==1.0" exits 0
pytest tests/services/test_metrics.py -v exits 0
mypy app/services/metrics.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Implemented app/services/metrics.py (stdlib-only) exposing emit_metric(namespace, metric_name, value, unit='Count', dimensions=None, **extra), emit_metrics(namespace, metrics=[{name,value,unit}], dimensions=None, **extra), and @contextmanager timed(namespace, metric_name, dimensions=None, unit='Milliseconds', **extra). Each helper prints one compact EMF JSON line to stdout using print(..., flush=True) so the ECS awslogs driver auto-parses into CloudWatch metrics with zero SDK calls. Design notes: (1) Dimensions outer array always has length 1 per EMF schema (minItems:1); when no dimensions are passed, the inner DimensionSet is [] which EMF permits (minItems:0) â€” so empty emissions render as 'Dimensions': [[]]. (2) Dimension VALUES are always coerced to strings via str() because CloudWatch rejects non-string dimension targets; ints/floats/bools all work. (3) Timestamp is int(time.time()*1000), sampled at emit time (not function entry). (4) timed() wraps in try/finally so the metric is emitted even when the block raises, and the exception propagates. (5) timed(unit='Seconds') divides the elapsed ms by 1000 before emission; unit='Microseconds' multiplies by 1000; any other unit emits raw ms. (6) emit_metrics() with an empty metrics list raises ValueError (CloudWatch rejects empty Metrics arrays). Test file tests/services/test_metrics.py â€” 10 tests, all pytest + capfd-based, covering: basic shape + timestamp range, multi-metric variant, empty-list rejection, timed elapsed >= 45ms for a 50ms sleep, timed emits on exception, timed Seconds unit conversion, dimensions=None and dimensions={} both render [[]], non-string dimension coercion (int/float/bool), and single-compact-line invariant. Self-validation: pytest passed 10/10; mypy --follow-imports=silent exit 0; full regression 236 passed 1 skipped. Commit b326fb4. EMF spec fetched live from docs.aws.amazon.com â€” confirmed Dimensions is list-of-lists and Timestamp is ms-since-epoch int.
userStories.test_evidence: All artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/. Assertion 1 stdout (assertion1_smoke.log): one-line EMF JSON with _aws.CloudWatchMetrics[0]={Namespace:'SIG', Dimensions:[['Source']], Metrics:[{Name:'FeedLatency',Unit:'Seconds'}]}, top-level Source='SeekingAlpha', FeedLatency=1.5, exit=0. Assertion 2 (assertion2_heredoc.log): heredoc variant printed 'ok', exit=0. Assertion 3 (assertion3_pytest.log): pytest tests/services/test_metrics.py -v â†’ 10 passed in 0.15s, exit=0. Assertion 4 (assertion4_mypy.log): 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file', exit=0. Regression (regression.log): 236 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 6.56s, exit=0 â€” matches implementor's reported baseline exactly. Sanity (sanity_shape.log): Timestamp 1776462732790 is int-ms within 60s of now, Dimensions=[['Source']] (list-of-lists), Metrics=[{Name:'FeedLatency',Unit:'Seconds'}], top-level Source + FeedLatency present â†’ SHAPE_OK. Sanity (sanity_roundtrip.log): emit_metrics_multi OK, timed_elapsed OK (Dur=50.06ms), timed_exception_safe OK (exception propagates, metric still emitted), empty_dims_shape OK ([[]] rendered for dimensions=None).
userStories.test_report: US-001 PASS. All 4 prd-defined assertions pass (smoke one-liner, heredoc structural check, pytest 10/10, mypy scoped to metrics.py). Regression baseline 236 passed + 1 skipped reproduced â€” no new failures. Sanity checks confirm EMF wire shape matches the spec: _aws.Timestamp is an integer ms-since-epoch within 60s of current time; _aws.CloudWatchMetrics[0] has Namespace/Dimensions[[...]]/Metrics[{Name,Unit}] exactly; dimension values and metric values live at the top level as EMF targets. Exception-safe behavior of timed() confirmed â€” RuntimeError propagates and the metric line is still emitted. Multi-metric emit_metrics() and empty-dimensions fallback to Dimensions=[[]] both behave as specified. Artifact dir: jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/ (validate.sh + per-assertion logs + summary.log). No flags.

userStories.title: IAM + CDK â€” CloudWatch/CostExplorer read for admin task
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want the admin ECS task to have IAM permission to read CloudWatch metrics and Cost Explorer data, so the dashboard can call GetMetricData and GetCostAndUsage from the container.
acceptanceCriteria: infra/stack.py adds an IAM policy to the admin task role granting cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues on Resource:*
cdk diff shows only the IAM policy diff (no other resource churn)
Deploy applied via cdk deploy
A one-shot script running in the admin ECS task can call boto3 cloudwatch.list_metrics and ce.get_cost_and_usage without AccessDenied
cdk synth runs cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CE has no resource-level ARN support; scope=* is required. Cost Explorer calls cost $0.01/request â€” cache aggressively in US-007.
test_assertions: cd infra && npx cdk diff 2>&1 | grep -E 'IAM|PolicyDocument' matches expected additions
Inside admin ECS task: python3 -c "import boto3; boto3.client('cloudwatch').list_metrics(MaxResults=1)" exits 0
Inside admin ECS task: python3 -c "import boto3; boto3.client('ce').get_cost_and_usage(TimePeriod={'Start':'2026-04-10','End':'2026-04-17'}, Granularity='DAILY', Metrics=['UnblendedCost'])" exits 0
cd infra && npx cdk synth > /dev/null exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Added `aws_iam as iam` to the cdk imports in `infra/stack.py` and attached a single inline `iam.PolicyStatement` to `admin_task.task_role` (the runtime role that boto3 calls from the admin container assume). Statement: Effect=Allow, Actions=[cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues], Resource=['*']. CE does not support resource-level ARNs so wildcard is required; CloudWatch GetMetricData/ListMetrics also intentionally wildcarded to match the namespaces the dashboard will query (InstantNews/Ingestion, InstantNews/AIPipeline, InstantNews/Twitter, etc.) â€” narrowing is not useful here because resource-ARN scoping for GetMetricData isn't granular enough to gate per-namespace anyway. Self-validation: (1) `cd infra && npx cdk synth > /tmp/us002_synth.log 2>&1` â†’ exit 0. (2) `cd infra && npx cdk diff` â†’ exit 0 and diff is exactly one IAM Statement addition (AdminTask/TaskRole/DefaultPolicy) on Resource=* with the four listed actions, plus the automatic DependsOn wiring CDK adds to resources that reference the admin task role (AdminService/Service, its SecurityGroup, and its SecurityGroupIngress all gain `AdminTaskTaskRoleDefaultPolicy80ADD05C` alongside the pre-existing `AdminTaskTaskRole229B8B1F`). That DependsOn churn is cosmetic â€” no resource is replaced, no physical ID changes. Full diff captured at /tmp/us002_diff.log. **DEPLOY DEFERRED**: `cdk deploy` was NOT run from this agent â€” team lead batches all stack deploys at the end of the dashboard PRD. Post-deploy assertion (boto3 calls from admin task) can only run after that deploy lands; tester should treat that assertion as deferred and validate synth/diff shape only.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/. synth.log: `npx cdk synth` EXIT=0, template built cleanly. diff.log: `npx cdk diff` EXIT=0. IAM Statement Changes table shows one `+` row with Resource=`*`, Effect=Allow, Action=[ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues, cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics], Principal=AWS:${AdminTask/TaskRole}. Resources section: one new AWS::IAM::Policy (AdminTask/TaskRole/DefaultPolicy AdminTaskTaskRoleDefaultPolicy80ADD05C) plus DependsOn-only churn on AdminService/Service, its SecurityGroup, and the matching SecurityGroupIngress (each gains AdminTaskTaskRoleDefaultPolicy80ADD05C alongside the pre-existing AdminTaskTaskRole229B8B1F) â€” cosmetic, no physical ID replacement. diff_iam_actions.log: grep confirms all four actions present. Number of stacks with differences: 1.
userStories.test_report: US-002 TEST_PASSED. Synth + diff both exit 0 on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 1b84633. The IAM Statement diff is exactly one additive row on Resource=* containing all four required actions (cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues), attached to AdminTask/TaskRole via a new AdminTaskTaskRoleDefaultPolicy80ADD05C Policy resource. Cosmetic DependsOn churn on AdminService/Service + SecurityGroup + SecurityGroupIngress is accepted per implementor note â€” no resource replacement, no logical-ID change. Deferred (post-deploy): (a) boto3.client('cloudwatch').list_metrics(MaxResults=1) from inside admin ECS task â€” must return without AccessDenied; (b) boto3.client('ce').get_cost_and_usage(TimePeriod={'Start':<today-7d>,'End':<today>}, Granularity='DAILY', Metrics=['UnblendedCost']) from inside admin ECS task â€” must return without AccessDenied. Both require the team-lead's batched `cdk deploy`; re-run them once the policy is live. Node v18 EoL warning surfaced in synth/diff output is pre-existing, unrelated to this story.

userStories.title: Instrument per-source pollers with EMF metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want every source_poller tick to emit EMF metrics (latency, item count, fetch status) so the dashboard can render per-source health.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/source_poller.py _run_once emits one EMF line per tick with NewItems, IngestLatencySeconds (median fetched_at-published over new rows), FetchDurationMs dimensioned by {Source, SourceType}
On fetch exception emits FetchErrors=1 with dimension ErrorType
Namespace InstantNews/Ingestion
Per-source intervals unchanged from prior stories
aws cloudwatch list-metrics --namespace InstantNews/Ingestion shows >= 3 metric names across >= 3 source dimensions after soak
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. EMF is log-driven â€” no extra CloudWatch cost beyond existing log ingestion.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks fetch_feed with 2 items + known published timestamps; runs _run_once; captures stdout; asserts exactly one EMF JSON line with NewItems=2 and IngestLatencySeconds within range

Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Ingestion --metric-name NewItems --dimensions Name=Source,Value=SeekingAlpha --start-time $(date -u -d '-10 min' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 60 --statistics Sum returns >= 1 datapoint
mypy app/services/source_poller.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Modified `app/services/source_poller._run_once` to emit one multi-metric EMF line per successful tick and a single `FetchErrors=1` line on fetch exceptions. Added two module-private helpers: `_parse_iso_utc(ts)` accepts both `...+00:00` and trailing-`Z` ISO-8601 forms (every source in this repo emits the former via feed_parser.utc_iso, but EDGAR/others may produce Z â€” handle both) and returns `Optional[datetime]`; `_median_ingest_latency_seconds(items, fetched_at_iso)` skips rows with unparseable published timestamps, clamps negative deltas (clock skew) to 0, and returns `None` if no parseable rows remain. Metric emission rules: (a) successful tick with >= 1 new row emits `NewItems` (Count), `FetchDurationMs` (Milliseconds), and `IngestLatencySeconds` (Seconds) in ONE emit_metrics() call with dimensions `{Source: spec.name, SourceType: spec.label}`; (b) successful tick with zero items emits `NewItems=0` + `FetchDurationMs` only â€” latency is omitted (undefined for no rows) rather than emitting 0 so the dashboard's p50/p95 aggregations aren't polluted; (c) fetch exception emits one `FetchErrors=1` (Count) line with dimensions `{Source, SourceType, ErrorType}` where ErrorType is `exc.__class__.__name__`, and _store_items is NOT called. Wall-time `FetchDurationMs` uses `time.monotonic()` deltas *1000 around spec.fetch() (manual approach â€” `timed()` context manager couldn't be used because the value feeds into a later emit_metrics() call, not its own emit). Namespace is constant `InstantNews/Ingestion`. Per-source interval machinery in `start_source_thread` untouched. Restored the `@dataclass SourceSpec` block â€” it was accidentally dropped during the initial edit and caught immediately by the unit tests (module-level AttributeError on import). Self-validation: `pytest tests/services/test_source_poller_metrics.py -v` â†’ 3 passed (happy path 2 rows + median=60s, RuntimeError path emits FetchErrors with ErrorType=RuntimeError, zero-items path emits NewItems=0 without latency). `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/source_poller.py` â†’ Success, no issues. Full regression `pytest tests/ --tb=no -q` â†’ 239 passed, 1 skipped (236 baseline + 3 new). No Post-deploy assertion run â€” CloudWatch list-metrics assertion requires the deferred cdk deploy.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-003/attempt-1/. pytest.log: `pytest tests/services/test_source_poller_metrics.py -v` â†’ 3 passed in 0.02s, EXIT=0. Tests: test_run_once_emits_newitems_latency_duration (2-row happy path, emits Namespace=InstantNews/Ingestion, Dimensions=[['Source','SourceType']], Metrics={NewItems=2, FetchDurationMs>=0, IngestLatencySecondsâ‰ˆ60.0 median of 30s+90s), test_run_once_emits_fetch_errors_on_exception (RuntimeError branch emits FetchErrors=1 with Dimensions={Source, SourceType, ErrorType=RuntimeError}), test_run_once_emits_zero_items_without_latency (empty-fetch emits NewItems=0 + FetchDurationMs only â€” IngestLatencySeconds intentionally omitted so it doesn't pollute p50/p95 aggregations on quiet sources). mypy.log: `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/source_poller.py` â†’ Success: no issues found in 1 source file, EXIT=0. Full regression (US-005/regression.log): 246 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning â€” exact match to implementor baseline.
userStories.test_report: US-003 TEST_PASSED. pytest 3/3 green, mypy clean on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit f3421bb. EMF shape verified by reading the test file: namespace `InstantNews/Ingestion`, dimensions `{Source, SourceType}` on the happy+empty paths and `{Source, SourceType, ErrorType}` on the failure path, metric names `NewItems` (Count), `FetchDurationMs` (Milliseconds), `IngestLatencySeconds` (Seconds), and `FetchErrors` (Count) â€” all match the acceptance criteria. Both non-empty and empty-fetch paths covered, plus exception path with ErrorType dimension populated from exc.__class__.__name__. Deferred (post-deploy): `aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Ingestion --metric-name NewItems --dimensions Name=Source,Value=SeekingAlpha --start-time $(date -u -d '-10 min' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 60 --statistics Sum` must return >= 1 datapoint once the admin task is deployed and RSS pollers have run. Namespace in code (`InstantNews/Ingestion` at app/services/source_poller.py:25) matches the query namespace. Dimension shape (Source=<spec.name>, SourceType=<spec.label>) matches the query dimension filter.

userStories.title: Instrument AI pipeline with EMF metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want the AI pipeline to emit queue depth, batch duration, and backend-choice counters so the dashboard shows when MiniMax is rate-limited and falling back to Claude.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/ai_pipeline.py emits QueueDepth gauge once per dispatcher loop iteration
app/services/bedrock_analysis.py emits per-article BackendChosen counter with dimension {Backend: minimax|claude|bedrock} after each _call_model
Emits BatchSize and BatchDurationMs on each analyze_articles_batch completion
Namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Watch for emission overhead on high-throughput path â€” keep it to one log line per batch.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks Bedrock clients to simulate MiniMax failure -> Claude success; captures stdout; asserts EMF line has BackendChosen with Backend=claude
pytest tests/services/test_ai_pipeline_metrics.py -v exits 0
Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline --metric-name QueueDepth --start-time ... --statistics Maximum returns datapoints
mypy app/services/ai_pipeline.py app/services/bedrock_analysis.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Instrumented three emission points â€” all under namespace `InstantNews/AIPipeline`. **(1) QueueDepth in `_dispatcher_loop`**: emitted at the top of each outer loop iteration via `emit_metric('InstantNews/AIPipeline', 'QueueDepth', _queue.qsize(), 'Count', dimensions=None)` (no dimensions â€” it's a singleton gauge). Rate-limited by a local `last_queue_depth_emit` float: subsequent emissions are skipped until >= 5 s have elapsed since the last one. The default `batch_window_seconds=1.5` would otherwise fire this ~40 times/min per dispatcher; 5 s cap keeps the metric at ~12 emissions/min which is plenty for a 10-s dashboard refresh. **(2) BackendChosen in `_call_model`**: moved into a module-local `_emit_backend_chosen(backend)` helper that emits `BackendChosen=1` with dimension `{Backend: 'minimax'|'claude'|'bedrock'}`. Emission is BEFORE the `return` in each successful branch and NEVER on the failure side of the try/except (the signal is which backend actually served, not which were attempted). Bedrock branch, being the last-resort unconditional call, emits unconditionally after `_call_bedrock()` returns. **(3) BatchSize + BatchDurationMs in `analyze_articles_batch`**: wrapped the ThreadPoolExecutor block in a `time.monotonic()` stopwatch and emitted ONE multi-metric line on completion via `emit_metrics(namespace, metrics=[{BatchSize, Count}, {BatchDurationMs, Milliseconds}], dimensions=None)`. Early-return path (empty articles or BEDROCK_ENABLED=false) stays silent â€” otherwise every benign startup tick would emit a BatchSize=0 data point. Added `import time` and `from app.services.metrics import emit_metric, emit_metrics` to bedrock_analysis.py; added `from app.services.metrics import emit_metric` to ai_pipeline.py. Module-level constants `_AI_NAMESPACE = 'InstantNews/AIPipeline'` and `_QUEUE_DEPTH_MIN_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 5.0` keep magic strings out of the hot path. Self-validation: `pytest tests/services/test_ai_pipeline_metrics.py -v` â†’ 4 passed (Claude-served on MiniMax-absent, Bedrock-served on both-fail, BatchSize=1 emission shape, empty-batch silence). `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/ai_pipeline.py app/services/bedrock_analysis.py` â†’ Success, no issues. Full regression 243 passed 1 skipped (236 baseline + 3 US-003 + 4 US-004). NOTE: `_dispatcher_loop` was NOT unit tested directly because it's a `while not _stop_event.is_set()` loop with module-level globals â€” exercising it cleanly would need a subprocess or extensive monkeypatching of the module state. The QueueDepth emission is simple enough (single `emit_metric` call with a 5-s rate-limit) that the post-deploy CloudWatch assertion is the cheaper path to validate it.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-004/attempt-1/. pytest.log: `pytest tests/services/test_ai_pipeline_metrics.py -v` â†’ 4 passed in 0.02s, EXIT=0. Tests: test_call_model_emits_claude_backend (MiniMax returns None client â†’ Claude serves â†’ BackendChosen=1 with Backend=claude, Namespace=InstantNews/AIPipeline, Dimensions=[['Backend']]), test_call_model_emits_bedrock_backend_when_primaries_fail (MiniMax+Anthropic both raise â†’ Bedrock serves â†’ Backend=bedrock; exactly 1 EMF line confirms failures do NOT emit), test_analyze_articles_batch_emits_batch_metrics (BatchSize=1 + BatchDurationMs>=0 multi-metric line with Dimensions=[[]]), test_analyze_articles_batch_skips_emission_on_empty (empty batch stays silent, zero EMF lines). mypy.log: `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/ai_pipeline.py app/services/bedrock_analysis.py` â†’ Success: no issues found in 2 source files, EXIT=0. Dispatcher-loop QueueDepth emit confirmed by source inspection: app/services/ai_pipeline.py lines 20-60 â€” _AI_NAMESPACE='InstantNews/AIPipeline', _QUEUE_DEPTH_MIN_INTERVAL_SECONDS=5.0, emit_metric(namespace=_AI_NAMESPACE, metric_name='QueueDepth', value=_queue.qsize(), unit='Count', dimensions=None) gated by `now_mono - last_queue_depth_emit >= 5.0`.
userStories.test_report: US-004 TEST_PASSED. pytest 4/4 green, mypy clean on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 790e9cb. All three acceptance criteria covered: (a) BackendChosen emits Backend=claude on MiniMax-down / Claude-success, (b) BackendChosen emits Backend=bedrock on MiniMax+Claude-down / Bedrock-success, (c) analyze_articles_batch emits BatchSize + BatchDurationMs on a non-empty batch. Accepted limitation per handoff: _dispatcher_loop is not unit-tested (module-level infinite loop with globals) â€” instead verified by inspection that the QueueDepth emission lives at the top of the while-loop, uses namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline, and is rate-limited to once per 5 s via a monotonic-clock gate. Failures-don't-emit discipline confirmed by test 2: exactly 1 EMF line despite MiniMax+Anthropic both raising RuntimeError before Bedrock runs. Deferred (post-deploy): `aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline --metric-name QueueDepth --start-time <recent> --end-time <now> --period 60 --statistics Maximum` must return datapoints once the worker container is deployed and the dispatcher loop has ticked. Namespace in code matches the query namespace; QueueDepth has no dimensions, so the query must omit `--dimensions` to hit the datapoints.

userStories.title: Instrument X API calls with rate-limit + cost metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want every /2/tweets/search/recent response to emit rate-limit headroom + tweet/user counts so the dashboard shows X API health and live-estimated monthly spend.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/twitter_source.py TwitterClient._get on 200 emits EMF metrics: RateLimitRemaining (from x-rate-limit-remaining header), RateLimitLimit, TweetsBilled (len response.data), UsersBilled (len includes.users), dimension {Endpoint: search_recent}
On 429 emits RateLimited=1 with same dimension
Namespace InstantNews/Twitter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Don't disturb existing since_id cost-saving machinery.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks requests.Session.get returning 200 + headers + body; calls _get; asserts EMF line shape includes RateLimitRemaining and TweetsBilled values
Unit test mocks 429; asserts RateLimited=1 emitted
pytest tests/services/test_twitter_metrics.py -v exits 0

Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Twitter --metric-name TweetsBilled --statistics Sum --start-time $(date -u -d '-1h' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 300 returns datapoints matching observed call volume
mypy app/services/twitter_source.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Instrumented `TwitterClient.search_recent` in `app/services/twitter_source.py` to emit one EMF line per successful `/2/tweets/search/recent` response and one `RateLimited=1` line per 429 â€” both under namespace `InstantNews/Twitter` with dimension `{Endpoint: 'search_recent'}`. Implementation detail: emission happens INSIDE `search_recent` (not in the generic `_get` helper) because (a) the body is already parsed once into `data` there, reusing the parse for `TweetsBilled = len(data.get('data', []) or [])` and `UsersBilled = len((data.get('includes') or {}).get('users', []) or [])` â€” no double-parse, no body-reread; (b) `_get` is called by other endpoints (resolve_user_id) whose response shape and rate-limit model differ and we don't want to over-emit. Added a small `_header_int(resp, key)` helper that returns `Optional[int]` â€” safely parses `x-rate-limit-remaining` and `x-rate-limit-limit` headers, returning None if the header is missing, non-numeric, or the response object is a partial mock. On 200: emits a multi-metric line via `emit_metrics` with `[TweetsBilled, UsersBilled]` unconditionally plus `RateLimitRemaining` and `RateLimitLimit` ONLY when the headers are parseable (so tests that mock without headers still produce clean EMF lines without NaN poisoning). On 429: emits `RateLimited=1` via `emit_metric` right before the existing `break` that stops the chunk loop. Since-id semantics, chunking, user-id caching, and `_last_max_newest_id` persistence are untouched. Added `# type: ignore[import-untyped]` on the `import requests` line â€” `requests` lacks bundled stubs and mypy otherwise fails even without US-005 changes; the ignore is scoped to that single import and unrelated to the new code. Self-validation: `pytest tests/services/test_twitter_metrics.py -v` â†’ 3 passed (200 path w/ headers â†’ 4 metrics including RateLimitRemaining=449 & TweetsBilled=1, 429 path â†’ 1 RateLimited metric, 200 path without headers â†’ 2 metrics with graceful header omission). `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/twitter_source.py` â†’ Success. Composite mypy over all five touched service modules â†’ Success 5/5. Full regression 246 passed 1 skipped (baseline 236 + 3 US-003 + 4 US-004 + 3 US-005). Post-deploy CloudWatch assertion deferred until team lead's batched `cdk deploy`.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-005/attempt-1/. pytest.log: `pytest tests/services/test_twitter_metrics.py -v` â†’ 3 passed in 0.02s, EXIT=0. Tests: test_search_recent_emits_metrics_on_200 (200 + rate-limit headers â†’ 1 EMF line; Namespace=InstantNews/Twitter, Dimensions=[['Endpoint']], Endpoint='search_recent', Metrics={TweetsBilled=1, UsersBilled=1, RateLimitRemaining=449, RateLimitLimit=450}), test_search_recent_emits_rate_limited_on_429 (429 â†’ 1 EMF line with Metrics=[{RateLimited,Count}], RateLimited=1, loop breaks, zero tweets returned), test_search_recent_tolerates_missing_headers (200 without headers â†’ EMF line emits only TweetsBilled + UsersBilled; rate-limit metrics gracefully omitted). mypy.log: `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/services/twitter_source.py` â†’ Success: no issues found in 1 source file, EXIT=0 â€” `# type: ignore[import-untyped]` on requests import accepted per implementor note. regression.log: 246 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 6.60s, EXIT=0 â€” exact match to implementor baseline of 236+3+4+3.
userStories.test_report: US-005 TEST_PASSED. pytest 3/3 green, mypy clean on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 437ebc8. Both acceptance criteria covered: (a) 200 path emits RateLimitRemaining, RateLimitLimit, TweetsBilled, UsersBilled with dimension Endpoint=search_recent under namespace InstantNews/Twitter; (b) 429 path emits RateLimited=1 with the same dimension. Header-absent tolerance also validated (graceful omission, no NaN poisoning). Deferred (post-deploy): `aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Twitter --metric-name TweetsBilled --statistics Sum --start-time $(date -u -d '-1h' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 300` must return datapoints matching observed call volume once the web container is deployed and Twitter polling has run. Namespace in code (app/services/twitter_source.py:36 `_TWITTER_NAMESPACE = "InstantNews/Twitter"`) matches query namespace; dimension Endpoint=search_recent matches dimension shape â€” post-deploy query must include `--dimensions Name=Endpoint,Value=search_recent` to select the datapoint.

userStories.title: Admin API POST /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want one admin endpoint that proxies cloudwatch:GetMetricData so the dashboard can fetch multi-metric time series in one call.
acceptanceCriteria: New blueprint app/admin/metrics.py exposes POST /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch (admin role check)
Request JSON: {range: '1h'|'24h'|'7d', queries: [{id, namespace, metric, dimensions, stat}]}
Resolves range to start_time, end_time, period (1h->60s, 24h->60s, 7d->3600s)
Calls cloudwatch:GetMetricData with up to 500 queries per request
Response JSON: {series: {[id]: {timestamps, values}}}
Server-side in-memory cache (60s TTL) keyed by query hash
Unauthenticated request returns 401; non-admin returns 403
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 for IAM. Use POST because GetMetricData payload can be large.
test_assertions: curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_JWT' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"range":"1h","queries":[{"id":"q1","namespace":"InstantNews/Ingestion","metric":"NewItems","dimensions":{"Source":"SeekingAlpha"},"stat":"Sum"}]}' returns 200 with series.q1.timestamps array
curl without Authorization header returns 401
curl with non-admin JWT returns 403
Second identical call within 60s returns identical body (cache hit)
mypy app/admin/metrics.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Created new blueprint `app/admin/metrics.py` (url_prefix `/admin/api/metrics`) and registered it in `app/routes/__init__.py` next to the existing admin blueprint, gated by the same `ADMIN_ENABLED=true` env check. Reused the existing `@require_admin` decorator from `app.admin.auth` â€” it reads `g.current_user.role` and returns 401 if user is None, 403 if role is neither `admin` nor `superadmin`. No new session/auth logic. Endpoint body: (1) POST /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch, validates `range in {1h,24h,7d}` + `queries` is a non-empty list (cap 500 per AWS GetMetricData batch limit) + each query has `id`, `namespace`, `metric`, `stat`. (2) Resolves range via `_resolve_range`: 1h/24h -> period=60s, 7d -> period=3600s; `end=datetime.now(UTC)` and `start=end-range`. (3) Builds `MetricDataQueries` per boto3 shape with `MetricStat.{Metric:{Namespace,MetricName,Dimensions:[{Name,Value}]}, Period, Stat}` and `ReturnData=True`. Dimension values coerced via `str(v)` since CloudWatch rejects non-string dim targets. (4) Calls `boto3.client('cloudwatch').get_metric_data(MetricDataQueries=..., StartTime=start, EndTime=end, ScanBy='TimestampAscending')` via a `_cloudwatch_client()` factory indirection so tests can `patch("app.admin.metrics._cloudwatch_client", ...)`. (5) Maps `MetricDataResults` back to `{series: {[id]: {timestamps: [unix-seconds int], values: [float]}}}` â€” `Timestamps` from boto3 are `datetime` objects converted via `t.timestamp()`. Queries that CloudWatch doesn't echo back get an empty `{timestamps:[], values:[]}` entry (defensive). (6) 60-second TTL cache: custom thread-safe `_TTLCache` class (dict + threading.Lock) since `cachetools` isn't available in the base conda env. Cache key = sha256 of `json.dumps({range, queries}, sort_keys=True)`. On hit, returns payload with `X-Cache: HIT` header; on miss, `X-Cache: MISS`. (7) Error handling: `botocore.exceptions.ClientError` is caught and returns 502 with the error detail â€” doesn't leak stacktrace. Self-validation: `pytest tests/admin/test_metrics_cloudwatch.py -v` -> 8 passed. `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/admin/metrics.py` -> Success (only 2 benign annotation-unchecked notes on untyped-function bodies, no errors). Full regression after both US-006+US-007 = 261 passed + 1 skipped (baseline 246 + 15 new = 261 exact match). Commit `9e86d79`. Tester: endpoint is POST-only and reads JSON body; non-JSON bodies coerce to `{}` via `silent=True` and then fail the range validation with 400. Test pattern for 403: seed a User row with `role='user'` and matching `firebase_uid`, then `patch('app.auth.firebase.verify_id_token', return_value=MOCK_DECODED_TOKEN)` â€” the middleware loads the seeded user into `g.current_user` and `require_admin` rejects on role. For 200 tests, seed `role='admin'`. Same pattern works for US-007. The `_clear_metrics_cache` autouse fixture in tests/admin/ flushes all three TTL caches between tests so ordering is deterministic.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-006/attempt-1/. blueprint_smoke.log: `from app.admin.metrics import metrics_bp; print(metrics_bp.name)` -> `admin_metrics`, EXIT=0. routes_smoke.log: `create_app()` enumerates `['/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch', '/admin/api/metrics/cost']`, EXIT=0. routes_assert.log: asserts `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch in routes` -> `OK ['/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch', '/admin/api/metrics/cost']`, EXIT=0. pytest.log: `pytest tests/admin/test_metrics_cloudwatch.py -v` -> 8 passed in 0.36s, EXIT=0 â€” tests: test_happy_path_returns_series (mocks boto3 `_cloudwatch_client` returning MetricDataResults; verifies response shape `{series: {q1: {timestamps:[int], values:[3.0,5.0]}}}`, `X-Cache: MISS`, and the sent MetricDataQuery carries Namespace='InstantNews/Ingestion', Stat='Sum', Period=60, Dimensions=[{Name:'Source',Value:'SeekingAlpha'}]), test_missing_auth_returns_401 (no Authorization header -> 401), test_non_admin_returns_403 (seeded role='user' with mocked firebase verify -> 403), test_invalid_range_returns_400 (range='30d' -> 400), test_empty_queries_returns_400, test_missing_query_field_returns_400, test_cache_hit_within_60s_skips_boto3 (2 identical POSTs -> r1 X-Cache=MISS, r2 X-Cache=HIT, `fake.call_count == 1`), test_7d_range_uses_3600_period. mypy.log: `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/admin/metrics.py` -> `Success: no issues found in 1 source file`, EXIT=0 (2 benign annotation-unchecked notes on lines 184/220). Full regression in US-007/regression.log: 261 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning â€” exact match to the implementor's reported baseline.
userStories.test_report: US-006 TEST_PASSED. All 5 non-deferred assertions green on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 9e86d79. Assertion 1 (blueprint import): blueprint name `admin_metrics` printed. Assertion 2 (route enumeration): `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch` present in `app.url_map`. Assertion 3 (pytest 8 tests): all 8 pass â€” the required coverage set (valid POST w/ mocked boto3 shape, 401 no-auth, 403 non-admin, 400 invalid range, cache-hit on identical POST proving boto3 called exactly once) is fully present plus three additional validation tests (empty queries, missing query field, 7dâ†’3600s period). Assertion 4 (required coverage subset) already satisfied under assertion 3. Assertion 5 (mypy): clean. Sanity checks passed by source inspection (app/admin/metrics.py): range resolution 1h/24hâ†’60s, 7dâ†’3600s (lines 47-56); batch cap 500 with 400 rejection beyond (lines 103, 156-159); cache key = sha256 of json.dumps({range, queries}, sort_keys=True) (lines 171-174); `@require_admin` reused from `app.admin.auth` (lines 35, 124); blueprint registered inside `ADMIN_ENABLED=true` guard in app/routes/__init__.py:30-34. Deferred (post-deploy, not blocking): the live `curl http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch` assertions require the admin ECS task running with IAM creds â€” re-run after the team lead's batched `cdk deploy`.

userStories.title: Admin API GET /admin/api/metrics/cost
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want the dashboard to fetch authoritative cost figures (AWS Cost Explorer daily totals + X API usage) without the frontend holding IAM credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/api/metrics/cost?range=7d returns {aws:{by_service,daily_totals}, x_api:{used_this_month, quota, reset_at}}
AWS: ce.get_cost_and_usage(Granularity='DAILY', GroupBy=[{Type:'DIMENSION',Key:'SERVICE'}], Metrics=['UnblendedCost'])

X API: implementor MUST WebFetch current X docs to verify /2/usage/tweets availability on Basic tier; if unavailable, fall back to summing InstantNews/Twitter.TweetsBilled for the month * $0.005 with estimated=true flag in response
Cache: 1h TTL on Cost Explorer (response-level), 5 min on X usage
Admin role check; non-admin returns 403
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-1 resolution lives here. Cost Explorer = $0.01/request, cache discipline is mandatory.
test_assertions: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_JWT' 'http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cost?range=7d' returns 200 with keys aws and x_api
Second identical call within 1h returns response with header X-Cache: HIT
curl with non-admin JWT returns 403
Response x_api section includes either {used_this_month, quota, reset_at} or {estimated: true, used_this_month}
mypy app/admin/metrics.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Extended `app/admin/metrics.py` with `GET /admin/api/metrics/cost` (same blueprint, same `@require_admin`). Allowed `range` values are `24h|7d|30d` with a default of `7d`; CE's DAILY granularity excludes `End`, so `range=7d` asks for `[today-7, today]` which yields 7 daily buckets ending yesterday. AWS piece issues **two** `ce.get_cost_and_usage` calls: (a) daily totals (no grouping) yielding `[{date, cost}, ...]`, (b) by-service grouped `GroupBy=[{Type:DIMENSION,Key:SERVICE}]` where I sum across days per service and emit `[{service, cost}, ...]` sorted descending by cost. Both calls use `Metrics=['UnblendedCost']`. The boto3 client is obtained via `_ce_client()` indirection so tests can mock. Cache: custom `_TTLCache` keyed by `cost:<range>` with 1h TTL on the outer response (minimises CE spend at $0.01/call). **X API piece â€” OQ-1 resolution**: WebFetched `https://docs.x.com/x-api/fundamentals/rate-limits` which confirms `GET /2/usage/tweets` exists with a 50/15min per-app rate limit. Cross-referenced via X developer community + docs.x.com/x-api/fundamentals/post-cap which together document the response fields `project_cap`, `project_usage`, `cap_reset_day` and confirm Basic tier monthly cap of 10,000 posts. Primary path calls `https://api.x.com/2/usage/tweets` with the `X_API_BEARER_TOKEN` bearer (loaded from env, same secret used by `twitter_source.py`) via `_fetch_x_api_usage`. On 200 it returns `{used_this_month, quota, reset_at}` where `reset_at` is a UTC ISO string derived from `cap_reset_day` (defaults to next calendar-month-start if the field is missing or unparseable). **Fallback path**: on 401/403/404 status (endpoint unavailable on this tier or token lacks scope) OR on any network exception OR when `X_API_BEARER_TOKEN` is empty, calls `cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics` against `InstantNews/Twitter.TweetsBilled` with `Dimensions=[{Name:'Endpoint', Value:'search_recent'}]` and `StartTime=<month_start>`, `EndTime=<now>`, `Period=86400`, `Statistics=['Sum']`. Sums the `Datapoints[].Sum` values into `used_this_month` and returns `{estimated: true, used_this_month, estimated_cost_usd: round(used*0.005, 4)}`. The fallback also triggers if CloudWatch itself raises ClientError â€” in that case we return zeros rather than 5xx, since authoritative AWS cost is already in the response. **Separate 5-min cache on the X usage portion**: keyed `"x_usage"` via `_x_usage_cache` (independent from the 1h outer cost cache) so the outer cache doesn't drag a stale monthly-usage figure forward for a full hour. Response headers: `X-Cache: HIT|MISS` per spec. 401/403 via the same `@require_admin`. 400 on invalid `range`. Self-validation: `pytest tests/admin/test_metrics_cost.py -v` -> 7 passed. `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/admin/metrics.py` -> Success. Full regression 261 passed + 1 skipped (baseline 246 + 8 US-006 + 7 US-007). Commit `7b77b8d`. **X API docs finding for the team lead**: `/2/usage/tweets` IS available on Basic tier per the X docs rate-limits page (listed explicitly in the usage section with 50/15min per-app limit). Response fields confirmed via cross-referenced X community posts: `project_cap`, `project_usage`, `cap_reset_day`. OQ-1 therefore resolves to the **primary path** being the happy case in production â€” but the fallback path remains mandatory to gracefully handle 401/403 (if the bearer token's project doesn't have usage-endpoint scope) or future API changes. Both paths are unit-tested.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-007/attempt-1/. pytest.log: `pytest tests/admin/test_metrics_cost.py -v` -> 7 passed in 0.33s, EXIT=0 â€” tests: test_happy_path_with_x_api_200 (mocked `_ce_client` returns daily + by-service buckets; mocked `_fetch_x_api_usage` returns `{used_this_month:4321, quota:10000, reset_at:'2026-05-01T00:00:00Z'}`; response has shape `{aws: {by_service, daily_totals}, x_api: {used_this_month, quota, reset_at}}` with `X-Cache: MISS` and by_service sorted descending by cost), test_missing_auth_returns_401, test_non_admin_returns_403, test_x_usage_404_falls_back_to_cloudwatch_estimate (patches `requests.get` returning 404 + `_cloudwatch_client` returning TweetsBilled datapoints summing to 5500 -> response `x_api.estimated=true`, `used_this_month=5500`, `estimated_cost_usdâ‰ˆ27.5`), test_cache_hit_returns_hit_header (r1 X-Cache=MISS, r2 X-Cache=HIT), test_invalid_range_returns_400 (range='1h' rejected as 400 since cost endpoint only accepts 24h/7d/30d), test_no_bearer_token_uses_cloudwatch_estimate (empty X_API_BEARER_TOKEN -> falls through to CloudWatch estimator directly, estimated=true). mypy.log (shared with US-006): `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/admin/metrics.py` -> `Success: no issues found in 1 source file`, EXIT=0. regression.log: 261 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 7.15s â€” exact match to the baseline (246 + 8 US-006 + 7 US-007 = 261).
userStories.test_report: US-007 TEST_PASSED. All 3 non-deferred assertions green on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 7b77b8d. Assertion 1 (pytest 7 tests): all 7 pass. Assertion 2 required coverage fully present: valid GET with mocked CE+X usage returning shape `{aws:{by_service,daily_totals}, x_api:{...}}`, 401 no-auth, 403 non-admin, X-API 404 -> fallback `{estimated:true,...}`, cache-hit sends `X-Cache: HIT` header. Plus two additional tests (invalid range, missing bearer token). Assertion 3 (mypy, shared file with US-006): clean. Sanity checks passed: X API fallback path returns `estimated: true` on 401/403/404 / network exception / empty bearer (lines 389-392, 422, 484-487, 491-496, 497-506 of app/admin/metrics.py); cache keys deterministic (cost cache keyed `cost:<range>`, X cache keyed `x_usage` with independent 5-min TTL so a stale monthly quota can't persist for 1h); `@require_admin` reused; blueprint gated by `ADMIN_ENABLED=true` in app/routes/__init__.py. Deferred (post-deploy, not blocking): the live `curl 'http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cost?range=7d'` assertions require the admin ECS task running with IAM creds AND the US-002 IAM policy deployed â€” re-run after the team lead's batched `cdk deploy` for a real Cost Explorer call validation.

userStories.title: Dashboard page scaffold + shared components
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want the base /admin/monitoring page with navigation, dark theme CSS, time-range selector, and auto-refresh plumbing wired to the admin API so each panel story can drop in without re-building the shell.
acceptanceCriteria: New route GET /admin/monitoring (admin-only) serves HTML from frontend/src/admin-monitoring.js via Vite
Dark theme matching /terminal; reuses existing styles/ CSS tokens
Time-range selector with chips: 1h (Live), 24h, 7d; Live auto-refresh every 10s, 24h/7d every 60s
Shared fetchMetrics(queries, range) function POSTs to /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch
uPlot imported as charting library
Empty panel placeholders with section headers: Ingestion, AI Pipeline, Upstream APIs, Cost
No JS console errors on load
Typecheck passes
npm run build exits 0
Verify in browser: navigate to https://admin.instnews.net/monitoring over VPN, see shell + working time-range toggle
userStories.notes: uPlot ~40 KB; prefer over Chart.js or ECharts per FR-8.
test_assertions: Playwright: navigate to /admin/monitoring as admin user; assert selectors .panel--ingestion, .panel--ai, .panel--upstream, .panel--cost, .timerange-selector exist
Playwright: click 24h chip; intercept window.fetch; assert fetch polling interval is 60s
curl -I http://localhost:8000/admin/monitoring -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_JWT' returns 200
mypy app/routes/admin_monitoring.py exits 0
cd frontend && npm run build exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Built the dashboard shell (no panel content) â€” all panel stories US-009..US-013 will register into this scaffold via `window.__monitoring__.registerPanel(name, fn)`. **Route**: new `app/routes/admin_monitoring.py` exposes `GET /admin/monitoring` guarded by `@require_admin` from `app.admin.auth`. Registered in `app/routes/__init__.py` inside the existing `ADMIN_ENABLED=true` block next to `admin_metrics_bp`. Handler serves `static/admin-monitoring.html` via `send_from_directory(current_app.static_folder, ...)` â€” same pattern as `/terminal` in `static_pages.py`. Because `@require_admin` reads `g.current_user` populated by `app/auth/middleware.load_current_user`, the HTML route behaves like the JSON admin APIs: Bearer-token in the request â†’ user loaded â†’ 200 for role in {admin, superadmin}, 403 for other roles, 401 for no token. Verified all three paths via Flask test_client (smoke below). **HTML** (`frontend/admin-monitoring.html`): top bar with left-side title (InstNews Monitoring), centred `.timerange-selector` with three `.timerange-btn` elements (1h Live / 24h / 7d, `aria-pressed`+`.active` on the default), right-side `.mon-summary` counter starting at '0 criticals / 0 warnings'. Panel grid: `.panel.panel--ingestion` full-width, then a `.panel-row` containing `.panel--ai` + `.panel--upstream` side-by-side, then `.panel--cost` full-width. Each panel has a `.panel__header` with an `<h2>` and a `.panel__body > .placeholder` div that says 'Pending â€” populated by US-009/010/011/012'. Inter loaded from Google Fonts for body/labels, JetBrains Mono for numerics â€” identical stack to `terminal.html` so tokens are consistent. **CSS** (`frontend/src/styles/admin-monitoring.css`): redefines the `terminal.css` tokens at `:root` (--bg-primary, --bg-surface, --text-primary/secondary, --green/--red/--yellow etc.) locally because monitoring is a standalone Vite entry that does not import terminal.css â€” copying the token block (vs. importing terminal.css) keeps the bundle small and avoids dragging in the 45KB terminal rules. 4px spacing grid (--sp-1..--sp-6), 4px radius, 1px subtle borders, sticky top bar, 1600px max-width main. Dark theme matches terminal exactly (--bg-primary #0d1117, --text-primary #e6edf3). Media query collapses panel-row to single column under 900px. **JS** (`frontend/src/admin-monitoring.js`): imports uPlot (`^1.6.32`) + `uplot/dist/uPlot.min.css` so bundle resolves â€” no chart instantiated in US-008. Exports `TIME_RANGES = ['1h','24h','7d']`, `POLL_INTERVALS = {1h:10_000, 24h:60_000, 7d:60_000}`, `setRange(range)`, `fetchMetrics(queries, range)` (POST to `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch` via `SignalAuth.fetch`), `fetchCost(range)` (GET `/admin/api/metrics/cost`). A `panels` object registry + `registerPanel(name, refreshFn)` lets each subsequent panel story drop in a callback that `refreshAllPanels()` invokes on every timer tick. `setRange` updates `.active` + `aria-pressed` on buttons, resets the interval (`clearInterval(state.pollTimer); setInterval(refreshAllPanels, POLL_INTERVALS[range])`), and calls `refreshAllPanels()` once immediately. All of this is exposed at `window.__monitoring__ = {state, setRange, registerPanel, refreshAllPanels, panels, fetchMetrics, fetchCost, updateSummary, uPlot, TIME_RANGES, POLL_INTERVALS}` for Playwright introspection. **Vite** (`frontend/vite.config.js`): added `'admin-monitoring': resolve(__dirname, 'admin-monitoring.html')` as a new input; added `/admin/monitoring` to the cleanRoutes rewrite so the dev server at :5173 also resolves the path. **uPlot version**: `1.6.32` (pulled latest via `npm view uplot version`, installed via `npm install uplot`). No `@types/uplot` package exists on npm (uPlot ships its own `.d.ts`). Self-validation: (1) `create_app()` enumerates `['/admin/monitoring']` in url_map. (2) `client.get('/admin/monitoring')` anon â†’ 401 (logged `admin_monitoring.monitoring_page 401 (0.2ms)`). (3) With `g.current_user` stubbed as role='admin' â†’ 200 text/html serving the built HTML. (4) `cd frontend && npm run build` â†’ exit 0, writes `static/admin-monitoring.html` (4.29 KB) + `static/assets/admin-monitoring-BXfRzdQ6.js` (54.59 KB, gzipped 24.39 KB â€” uPlot included) + `static/assets/admin-monitoring-DwFTcpN6.css` (5.79 KB). (5) `grep -oE '(panel--ingestion|panel--ai|panel--upstream|panel--cost|timerange-selector|timerange-btn)'` against the built HTML returns all six. (6) Full regression `pytest tests/` â†’ 261 passed, 1 skipped â€” exact match to US-007 baseline, no new failures. (7) `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/routes/admin_monitoring.py` â†’ Success, no issues. Commit `6bb7bd2`. **Note on deferred post-deploy browser check**: PRD assertion 5 (Playwright at https://admin.instnews.net/monitoring over VPN) will happen in the tester stage â€” local Flask + Vite are the self-validation surface here.
userStories.test_evidence: Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-008/attempt-1/. **Assertion 1 (auth gating)** â€” routes.log: `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/admin/monitoring` (no Authorization) -> STATUS=401 CT=application/json; `curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-JWT>' ...` -> STATUS=200 CT=text/html (served 97-line admin-monitoring.html, see admin_body.html). Admin user seeded in SQLite at row id=25 (`admin-us008@test.local`, role='admin'); JWT minted with sub=str(user_id) using APP_JWT_SECRET from .env (setup_admin.log). **Assertion 2 (selectors)** â€” selectors.log: built `static/admin-monitoring.html` contains each of panel--ingestion, panel--ai, panel--upstream, panel--cost, timerange-selector exactly once; same 5 selectors present in the auth-served admin_body.html. **Assertion 3 (POLL_INTERVALS shape)** â€” eval_initial.log via Playwright: `window.__monitoring__.POLL_INTERVALS` == `{'1h':10000,'24h':60000,'7d':60000}` (minified in bundle as 1e4/6e4 per bundle_strings.log). **Assertion 4 (24h chip click)** â€” eval_after_click.log: clicking `[data-range='24h']` flips `window.__monitoring__.state.range` from '1h' to '24h'; btn.classList contains 'active'; aria-pressed='true'. eval_state.log confirms state.range='24h' + pollTimerActive=true after click. **Assertion 5 (uPlot bundled)** â€” eval_initial.log: `typeof window.__monitoring__.uPlot === 'function'`. **Assertion 6 (zero console errors on load)** â€” console_all_warm.log: on warm reload (region cached in localStorage) 3 messages total, 0 errors, 0 warnings (just 2 `[monitoring] refresh triggered 1h` debug lines + one DOM a11y VERBOSE note about password forms). On a cold first visit (console_all_pass2.log) Chromium logs a single 400 network error from `https://www.googleapis.com/identitytoolkit/v3/relyingparty/getProjectConfig?key=test` â€” this is auth.js `_detectRegion()` probing Google reachability in a try/catch that intentionally swallows the failure to classify the region as 'global'; same error occurs on /admin, /terminal, /account whenever localStorage.signal_auth_region is unset. Not caused by US-008 code. **Assertion 7 (mypy)** â€” mypy.log: `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/routes/admin_monitoring.py` -> `Success: no issues found in 1 source file`, EXIT=0. **Assertion 8 (npm build)** â€” build.log: `vite build` EXIT=0, emits `static/admin-monitoring.html` (4.29 KB), `static/assets/admin-monitoring-BXfRzdQ6.js` (54.59 KB / gzip 24.39 KB â€” uPlot bundled), `static/assets/admin-monitoring-DwFTcpN6.css` (5.79 KB). Playwright screenshot at playwright-dashboard-24h.png shows dark-theme shell with 24h chip highlighted green, four placeholder panels each reading `Pending â€” populated by US-009/010/011/012`, and top-right `0 criticals / 0 warnings` summary counter.
userStories.test_report: US-008 TEST_PASSED. All 8 assertions green on branch jojo/admin-monitoring-dashboard @ commit 6bb7bd2. **Path taken**: local Flask dev server on 127.0.0.1:8765 with `ADMIN_ENABLED=true` + `.env` loaded via python-dotenv (Docker stack brought up was unnecessary â€” the Flask run path alone exercises the same `register_routes` + `@require_admin` guard as the admin ECS service). Admin user seeded directly in the local SQLite DB (`data/news_terminal.db`); JWT minted via `jwt.encode({'sub': str(user.id), 'provider': 'email', ...}, APP_JWT_SECRET)` â€” note `sub` is `str(user_id)` not `firebase_uid` because `app/auth/middleware._try_app_jwt` parses `int(claims['sub'])` and does a DB lookup by primary key (NOT the path the tester setup doc originally suggested â€” that's a gotcha for future panel testers: app JWT uses numeric DB user_id in `sub`, not firebase_uid). Anon curl -> 401 (middleware returns JSON error), authed curl -> 200 with HTML. For the browser-runtime assertions the Playwright-MCP transport can't set Authorization headers on top-level navigation, so the built `static/admin-monitoring.html` + `/assets/admin-monitoring-*.js` bundle was served via `python -m http.server 8766` (unauthed, but the JS shell itself is auth-agnostic â€” it only attempts `SignalAuth.fetch` on registered panels, of which there are zero in US-008). This proved the JS shell exposes `window.__monitoring__` with the correct POLL_INTERVALS/TIME_RANGES/state/uPlot keys, that the 24h chip flips state.range as specified, and that zero console errors fire on warm reload. **The single cold-load 400 error** from auth.js `_detectRegion()` is pre-existing cross-cutting behaviour (swallowed in try/catch, affects all auth.js-importing pages) and is therefore not attributable to US-008; subsequent navigations within the same origin produce a clean 0-error console. **Mypy** on `app/routes/admin_monitoring.py` clean. **Build** exits 0 and produces the expected asset triplet. **Screenshot** (playwright-dashboard-24h.png) visually confirms the dark-theme shell matches the terminal aesthetic with proper chip highlighting and placeholder panel rendering. Panel stories US-009..US-013 now have a validated scaffold (`window.__monitoring__.registerPanel(name, fn)`) to drop into. Flags for future panel testers: (a) `sub` claim carries DB user_id (str), not firebase_uid â€” mint tokens accordingly; (b) if you see a 1-time 400 GET to `googleapis.com/getProjectConfig?key=test` in console, it's the auth.js region probe, not your panel code; pre-seed `localStorage.signal_auth_region='global'` to suppress it; (c) the Playwright-MCP transport lacks `setExtraHTTPHeaders`, so for end-to-end testing via the authed Flask route you either (i) inject `signal_app_token` into localStorage before nav + let SignalAuth.fetch attach it to panel API calls or (ii) fall back to the unauthed static-server approach used here, since the panel JS only hits `/admin/api/metrics/...` from `SignalAuth.fetch`, not from the navigation itself.

userStories.title: Ingestion panel â€” per-source latency tiles + sparklines
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see per-source ingestion latency p50/p95 + new-item rate as small tiles with sparklines so I can spot a slow source at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: panel--ingestion renders one tile per source (17 tiles: 15 RSS + Twitter + TruthSocial)

Tile shows: source name + rss|social badge, p50 latency (seconds) with green/yellow/red badge (thresholds <30s green, <120s yellow, >=120s red), p95 in small text, items/min sparkline (last 60 points)
Tiles sort by p95 desc (worst first)
Live mode: tiles recompute every 10s with smooth CSS value transitions
Click a tile to pin: right-side detail drawer opens with 24h latency uPlot line chart for that source
No JS console errors
Typecheck passes
npm run build exits 0
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 producing InstantNews/Ingestion metrics and US-008 scaffold.
test_assertions: Playwright: navigate to /admin/monitoring; wait for ingestion panel populate; assert tile count >= 15
Playwright: assert >= 1 tile has data-source-type='social' and >= 10 have data-source-type='rss'
Playwright: click first tile; assert detail drawer opens with canvas element
Playwright: intercept /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch response; verify request payload includes metric IngestLatencySeconds with Source dimension
cd frontend && npm run build exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Implemented ingestion panel in `frontend/src/admin-monitoring.js` (+ shared helpers in `frontend/src/admin-monitoring-helpers.js`). On boot the panel fetches `/api/sources` once (via `SignalAuth.fetch`) to derive the tile list â€” each source carries a `source_type` of 'rss' or 'social' (falling back to a name-prefix match on `Twitter/@` or `TruthSocial/@`). For each refresh tick it POSTs one batch to `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch` with three queries per source: NewItems Sum, IngestLatencySeconds p50, IngestLatencySeconds p95 (CloudWatch accepts the `p50`/`p95` stat strings directly). Tiles render source name + rss|social pill badge + colored status dot (green <30s p50, yellow >=30s, red >=120s â€” thresholds from the story spec) + p95 in muted secondary text + an inline SVG `<polyline>` sparkline of the last 60 NewItems points. Tiles are re-sorted by p95 desc on each refresh (worst first) via a dataset.p95 read. Clicking a tile opens a right-side `<aside class='mon-drawer'>` with a 24h p50+p95 uPlot line chart for that source (uPlot earns its keep here where the static sparklines don't need it). Clicking the same tile again, pressing Escape, or clicking the Ã— closes the drawer. CSS additions in `frontend/src/styles/admin-monitoring.css` cover the tile grid, status dot/border states, and drawer transitions. Self-validation: `cd frontend && npm run build` exits 0 emitting `static/assets/admin-monitoring-*.js`; `grep -c 'ingest-tile' static/assets/admin-monitoring-*.js` > 0. No backend changes â€” only frontend wiring against existing `/api/sources` and `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch`. Backend pytest stays at 261 passed. Tester note: locally the CloudWatch endpoint returns empty series (no real AWS creds in dev) so tiles render as 'â€“' with no console errors â€” mock the fetchMetrics response via `window.__monitoring__.refreshIngestion(range)` after stubbing `fetchMetrics`, or pre-seed a series into `_ingestionState` via `window.__monitoring__.fetchMetrics = ...` before registration.

userStories.title: AI pipeline panel â€” queue depth + batch duration + fallback chain

acceptanceCriteria: panel--ai shows three widgets: queue depth gauge (live) + 1h sparkline, batch duration line chart (p50 + p95 over range), fallback-chain donut (% per backend)
Widgets update on dashboard's active refresh interval
If MiniMax share < 80% show yellow warning banner 'MiniMax fallback active â€” check credits'
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 producing InstantNews/AIPipeline metrics and US-008 scaffold.
test_assertions: Playwright: assert panel has .widget--queue-depth, .widget--batch-duration, .widget--fallback-chain
Playwright: mock /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch to return 50% Bedrock share; assert yellow warning banner appears
Playwright: assert donut renders three arcs with data-backend attributes minimax, claude, bedrock
cd frontend && npm run build exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Implemented AI pipeline panel alongside the ingestion panel in `frontend/src/admin-monitoring.js`. Renders three widgets inside `.panel--ai`: `.widget--queue-depth` (big current number + 160Ã—32 inline-SVG sparkline of the last 60 QueueDepth Max samples), `.widget--batch-duration` (uPlot line chart with two series â€” p50 and p95 BatchDurationMs), and `.widget--fallback-chain` (SVG donut with three arcs carrying `data-backend='minimax|claude|bedrock'` attributes using `stroke-dasharray` on concentric circles â€” no third-party chart library). One batched POST to `/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch` collects six queries: QueueDepth Maximum (no dimensions â€” bedrock_analysis emits without any dims), BatchDurationMs p50+p95 (no dims), and BackendChosen Sum Ã— 3 dimensioned by `Backend=minimax|claude|bedrock`. Backend shares are computed as `sum(BackendChosen)[k] / total` over the selected range; when `total > 0 && minimax share < 0.8`, a yellow `
MiniMax fallback active â€” check credits
` is shown at the top of the panel (removed when share >= 0.8 or total == 0 â€” no data shouldn't trigger a false alarm). Previous uPlot instance is destroyed on refresh to avoid leaks. CSS additions cover `.ai-widgets` grid, widget chrome, donut+legend layout, and the warn banner. Self-validation: `cd frontend && npm run build` exits 0, bundle includes the `.widget--queue-depth/.widget--batch-duration/.widget--fallback-chain/.ai-banner/banner--warn` selectors. Tester mock recipe: stub `window.__monitoring__.fetchMetrics` to return `{bmm: {values: [5]}, bcl: {values: [3]}, bbr: {values: [2]}}` (20% minimax) â†’ banner should appear. Return `{bmm: {values: [9]}, bcl: {values: [1]}, bbr: {values: [0]}}` (90% minimax) â†’ banner should hide.
